# Artifact Contract Template

**The frontmatter schema every artifact-producing skill writes. `manifest-sync.ts` indexes from these fields. Downstream eval skills read them. Get the contract wrong → indexing breaks, eval skills can't find their inputs, the chain fractures.**

This file is the canonical schema. Skills cite it instead of re-explaining frontmatter requirements. When the schema needs to change, change it here first, propagate via Phase 2 ripple coordination.

---

## Required fields (every artifact)

| Field | Type | Example |
|---|---|---|
| `skill` | kebab-case string | `eval-loop`, `copywriting`, `fresh-eyes` |
| `version` | integer (starts at 1) | `1`, `2` |
| `date` | ISO `YYYY-MM-DD` | `2026-05-16` |
| `status` | enum | `done` \| `done_with_concerns` \| `blocked` \| `needs_context` |

These four fields are non-negotiable for new artifacts. `manifest-sync.ts` does NOT currently fail-fast on missing fields — it warns and falls back to inference (`skill` from path, `date` from mtime, `status` defaults to `done` or `done_with_concerns` if unknown value, `version` defaults to 1). Missing required fields produce indexable-but-degraded entries. **Strict validation is the target state** for Phase 3 once Phase 2 refactors bring all artifact frontmatter up to spec. Until then, treat missing required fields as a code-review issue at the producer skill, not a runtime fail.

The status enum maps to the [Completion Status Protocol](../CLAUDE.md#completion-status-protocol) — `done` means meets all requirements + passes critic gate; the others surface limitations explicitly rather than hiding them.

---

## Selection fields (every non-terminal artifact)

A "non-terminal" artifact is one another skill or future session will read. Selection fields tell consumers when to use it. Required when `lifecycle` ≠ `ephemeral`.

| Field | Type | Example |
|---|---|---|
| `summary` | one-line string | `"[loop] measurable improvement loop"` |
| `purpose` | one-line string | `"Operating program for a measurable strategy -> execution -> evaluation loop"` |
| `lifecycle` | enum (see below) | `loop`, `decision`, `snapshot`, `pipeline` |
| `use_when` | one-line string | `"Coordinating repeated strategy, execution, evaluation, and keep/discard decisions for this initiative"` |

`artifact-index.md` (the human-readable companion to manifest.json) is generated from these — write them as if a future agent is choosing between 30 artifacts and needs ~one line to decide.

---

## Lifecycle enum

**Canonical source: [`references/_shared/manifest-spec.md`](manifest-spec.md) § "Field semantics" — `lifecycle` line.** That file lists the full 15-value enum as `manifest-sync.ts` recognizes it. Skill authors should treat that line as authoritative.

Author-facing summary (the values you'll set in frontmatter):

| Lifecycle | Behavior | Typical producers |
|---|---|---|
| `canonical` | Edited in place, kept forever, human-curated | `brand-system` → `brand/`, `system-architecture` → `architecture/`, `icp-research` → `research/`, this ref itself |
| `loop` | Operating contract for a measurable initiative | `eval-loop` → `skills-resources/loops/{slug}/program.md` |
| `loop-context` | Local assumptions, baselines, source links | `eval-loop` → `context.md` |
| `strategy` | Loop-specific plans, hypotheses, briefs | Strategy skills inside a loop folder |
| `execution` | Publish-ready marketing/content assets | Marketing/content execution skills inside a loop folder |
| `evaluation` | Post-execution metric snapshots, scoring | `*-eval` skills inside a loop folder |
| `learning` | Promoted evidence-backed lessons for future cycles | `eval-loop` → `learnings.md` |
| `pipeline` | One-time skill output; regenerated by re-running | `copywriting`, `ad-copy`, `cold-outreach`, etc. → `.agents/skill-artifacts/mkt/...` |
| `decision` | Dated immutable record of strategic choice | `agents-panel` → `.agents/skill-artifacts/meta/decisions/[date]-<slug>.md` |
| `snapshot` | Dated point-in-time record | `fresh-eyes`, `cleanup-artifacts` → `.agents/skill-artifacts/meta/records/[date]-<slug>.md` |
| `spec` | Working-draft specs awaiting promotion | `discover` → `.agents/skill-artifacts/meta/specs/<slug>.md` |
| `ephemeral` | Working files; cleanup-artifacts default target | `.agents/skill-artifacts/.archive/` after cleanup |

Derived/inferred values (set by `manifest-sync.ts` or `cleanup-artifacts`, NOT set by skill authors):

- `anchor` — auto-applied to session-anchor artifacts (e.g., `roadmap.md`, `tasks.md`)
- `registry` — auto-applied to living-registry files inside `meta/records/` (`kind: registry` frontmatter)
- `archive` — auto-applied to anything under `.archive/` post-cleanup

`sketch` is a working-draft folder convention (`meta/sketches/`) not yet a tracked lifecycle value — historically conflated with `spec`. Phase 2 task-breakdown / cleanup-artifacts refactors will decide whether to add it to the enum.

`agent-skills/CLAUDE.md § "Lifecycle taxonomy"` is a maintainer-facing summary that should mirror this table; if drift appears, this file (and manifest-spec.md) win because CLAUDE.md is not shipped to users.

---

## Optional / context-dependent fields

| Field | When to include | Example |
|---|---|---|
| `do_not_use_when` | Sharpens selection — when the artifact is wrong fit | `"The work has no observable metric or attribution path"` |
| `upstream` | Names what produced or fed this artifact | `"operator intent, prior artifacts, metric baseline"` |
| `downstream` | Names what consumes this artifact | `"strategy skills, marketing/content execution skills, evaluation skills"` |
| `loop` | When `lifecycle` is loop / strategy / execution / evaluation / learning | `"q3-launch-shortform"` |
| `provenance` | When extracting from another source (Gate 7) | YAML block: `extracted_from: ...`, `extracted_at: YYYY-MM-DD` |
| `consumers` | When this artifact is read by multiple downstream skills | `"every creative marketing skill"` |
| `supersedes` | When this artifact replaces a prior one | `"2026-05-10-old-slug.md"` |
| `kind` | When marking a living registry inside `records/` | `"registry"` |

`provenance` is the Gate 7 chain-hardening field. When a refactor extracts content from another file, the provenance block makes the lineage queryable.

---

## Minimum viable example (terminal artifact)

A `fresh-eyes` report that won't be re-read:

```yaml
---
skill: fresh-eyes
version: 1
date: 2026-05-16
status: done
---

# Fresh-Eyes Review: <slug>

...
```

Four required fields. No selection fields because the artifact is one-shot (read once, archive).

---

## Full feature example (non-terminal, in a loop)

An `eval-loop` `program.md` for an initiative loop:

```yaml
---
skill: eval-loop
version: 1
date: 2026-05-16
status: done
summary: "[loop] tiktok hold-rate improvement loop"
purpose: "Operating program for measurable hold-rate strategy -> execution -> evaluation"
lifecycle: loop
loop: tiktok-hold-rate
use_when: "Coordinating repeated strategy, execution, evaluation, and keep/discard decisions for tiktok hold-rate"
do_not_use_when: "The work has no observable metric or attribution path"
upstream: "operator intent, prior cycle learnings, platform analytics baseline"
downstream: "short-form-brief, short-form-eval"
---
```

---

## Variation by lifecycle

**Decision artifact** (`agents-panel`):

```yaml
---
skill: agents-panel
version: 1
date: 2026-05-16
status: done
summary: "Decision: ship Conquis desktop $59 single-tier pricing"
purpose: "Operator-committed pricing decision after 3-perspective debate"
lifecycle: decision
use_when: "Reading the rationale behind the $59 single-tier choice"
upstream: "discover spec, market-research"
---
```

**Snapshot artifact** (`fresh-eyes`):

```yaml
---
skill: fresh-eyes
version: 1
date: 2026-05-16
status: done_with_concerns
summary: "Fresh-eyes on eval-loop refactor (FIXED 2 inline, 1 deferred)"
purpose: "Independent QA review of eval-loop body-diet refactor"
lifecycle: snapshot
use_when: "Tracing what was caught/missed in the eval-loop refactor session"
upstream: "eval-loop refactor commits"
---
```

**Pipeline artifact** (`copywriting`):

```yaml
---
skill: copywriting
version: 1
date: 2026-05-16
status: done
summary: "Hero copy variants for conquis-desktop landing v3"
purpose: "Three hero-section copy variants scored against rubric"
lifecycle: pipeline
use_when: "Reviewing or selecting between Conquis desktop hero copy candidates"
upstream: "brand-system BRAND.md, lp-brief"
downstream: "humanize (terminal pass)"
---
```

---

## Contract stability rule (Gate 4)

**The frontmatter shape is a contract.** Changing field names, removing required fields, or altering the lifecycle enum breaks downstream consumers:

- `manifest-sync.ts` indexing fails-fast
- Eval skills can't locate their inputs (e.g., `lp-eval` reads `lp-brief` frontmatter to validate version)
- `artifact-index.md` regeneration loses selection hints
- Historical artifact replay (eval replay during refactors) breaks

When the schema needs to change:
1. Change this template first
2. Open a coordinated change across all producing skills + manifest-sync.ts + eval consumers
3. Bump `version:` in every artifact written by changed producers
4. Run a full-stack regression sweep via the harness before merging

Do not change one skill's frontmatter without ripple coordination.

---

## How skills cite this ref

**In SKILL.md "Artifact Requirements" section:**

```
[See `references/_shared/artifact-contract-template.md` for the full frontmatter schema.]
Below is this skill's specific instance.
```

Then show only the fields that are skill-specific (typically `summary` template, `lifecycle` value, `upstream` / `downstream` values, custom fields). Don't restate the required four (`skill`, `version`, `date`, `status`) — those are universal.

**In agent prompts that produce artifacts:**

```
Write frontmatter per `references/_shared/artifact-contract-template.md`. This artifact is
lifecycle: <lifecycle>; required selection fields: summary, purpose, use_when.
```

---

## Anti-patterns

1. **Missing `status:`.** Every artifact must declare status. Defaulting to `done` silently breaks the Completion Status Protocol — operator can't tell ship-ready from partial output.
2. **Bare `lifecycle:` without selection fields.** If `lifecycle ≠ ephemeral` and `summary` / `purpose` / `use_when` are missing, `artifact-index.md` regen produces a "no description" row. Future agents can't choose this artifact intelligently.
3. **Mixed status values.** Don't invent new status values (`partial`, `wip`, `review`). The enum is fixed; use `done_with_concerns` for partials.
4. **Stale `version:`.** Bump version when re-writing an artifact in place (rare — usually create a new dated snapshot instead). Bumping signals "schema changed, re-validate" to downstream.
5. **Skipping `provenance:` on extracted content.** Gate 7 requires it. Without provenance, the chain-hardening audit can't reconstruct lineage when source material moves.
6. **`date:` set to artifact-edit time, not artifact-create time.** Date is the original snapshot date; if you're editing in place that's a sign you should be creating a new dated snapshot.
